candidate_under_diagnosis: gate-2 candidate 8 (run 1, PR #93): FAIL 0/5, seven failing cell-instances across six cells

protocol.outer_holdout_contact: none beyond the already-published per-seed scores read from runs/gate2_hazard_v8.json; the holdout (side A) is never simulated here
protocol.split: populace_dynamics.harness.panel.split_panel_by_person(panel.attrs, 'person_id', fraction=0.5, seed=s); side A = outer holdout, side B = train complement (this diagnostic simulates side B)
protocol.fit_simulate_machinery: scripts/run_gate2_candidate8.py (merged #93; chain-imports candidates 1-7), reused byte-for-byte on the train side
protocol.draw_rng_rule: numpy default_rng(5200 + k) for k in 0..19 (distinct from the gate's 4200 + seed); the gate seed fixes the split, k varies only the simulation RNG
protocol.train_side_statistic: |ln(r_candidate_B / rate_b)| per cell, rate_b = side B's own weighted empirical reference moment

question_1_marriage_pathway.question: mean_lifetime_marriages|male (chronic since c1): which pathway under-produces, by how much, and does the deficit concentrate in an age x origin cell the current tables average over?
question_1_marriage_pathway.method: Train-side. Per gate seed, fit candidate 8 on side B and simulate the SAME side B at the 20 draw seeds 5200+k; average the per-person pathway counts over draws, then over the 5 gate seeds. Reference = side B empirical episodes. Conditioned population = the mean_lifetime_marriages|sex denominator (ever-married, observed through 45). Pathway = first / after_divorce / after_widowhood x order (2nd vs 3rd+) x marriage-age band.
question_1_marriage_pathway.finding: The male marriage-count deficit (+0.065, ref 1.431 vs sim 1.366) is NOT in the modelled datable in-exposure pathways: the simulation exceeds the reference in-exposure marriage count (sim 1.365 vs ref 1.315, in-exposure deficit -0.050). The deficit is carried by the reference residual (0.116/person of marriages with no datable/scoreable in-exposure event) -- dominated by 'undatable_na_start' (0.062/person) -- which the hazard tables cannot generate and the origin-split remarriage cells average over. The largest datable per-cell deficit is after_divorced|2nd|25-34 (+0.014/person), a specific age x order x origin remarriage cell the aggregated tables pool away.

question_2_completed_fertility_c1970s.question: completed_fertility.c1970s: is the gap a parity-progression miss (share reaching parity 2+), a timing miss aliasing into the completion window, or a censoring artifact the reference construction shares? Direction and magnitude, not guesses.
question_2_completed_fertility_c1970s.method: Train-side. c1970s women observed through 45 in side B; parity survival shares and mother-age-at-birth profile, reference vs the 20-draw-averaged simulation, per gate seed then averaged. mean_parity == sum_k P(parity>=k), so the survival-share deltas decompose the mean-parity gap by progression margin.

censoring_convention.reference_construction: completed parity = all birth-type events of women observed through 45, any mother age (transitions.build_fertility_panel all_births, no age window)
censoring_convention.simulation_construction: births generated only at mother age in [15, 49] (candidate 1 _ASFR_LO/_ASFR_HI)

question_2_completed_fertility_c1970s.finding: The c1970s completed-fertility gap: simulation UNDER-produces completed parity by 0.122 child/woman (ref 2.121 vs sim 1.999). Decomposing the mean gap by progression margin (mean parity == sum_k P(parity>=k)), it is a PARITY-PROGRESSION miss -- but a BROAD, LOW-parity one, NOT the 'share reaching 2+' story: the per-margin deficits are 0->1 +0.036, 1->2 +0.035, 2->3+ +0.020, 3+->4+ +0.014, declining monotonically with parity. The two low-parity steps (0->1 and 1->2) carry 58% of the gap (the first-birth (parity 0->1) progression the single largest at 29%); the model runs low at every birth but most at the first two. It is NOT a censoring artifact -- only 0.24% of reference cohort births fall outside the simulation's [15,49] window (convention effectively shared) -- and the age-at-birth profile barely shifts (mean mother age ref 27.90 vs sim 27.76), so timing is not the driver.

question_3_rng_stability.question: share_divorced.45-54|female (0.0003 over) and widowhood.75+|female / share_widowed.75+|female (seed-specific): per-seed score distributions under 20 additional RNG draws of the SAME candidate-8 spec on train-side simulations -- how much of each remaining clip is draw noise vs level?
question_3_rng_stability.method: Train-side, the c10-diagnostics analogue at simulation-RNG level (not split level). Per gate seed, fit candidate 8 on side B and simulate the SAME side B at 20 RNG seeds 5200+k (k=0..19); score each cell train-side |ln(r_candidate_B / rate_b)| against side B's own empirical rate. Per-cell mean/sd across the 20 draws. The published OUTER (side-A holdout) score is READ from the committed candidate-8 artifact runs/gate2_hazard_v8.json -- the holdout is never re-simulated.

summary.verdict_detail: NOISE-DOMINATED: a small, reproducible train-side under-production tilt (-0.103, same sign on all 5 seeds) sits well inside the tolerance 0.212 (level 0.103 = 49% of tol; P(a fresh train draw clips) 0.03). The outer clip on seed(s) [0] is dominated by split (rate_a vs rate_b) + single-draw excess, not the level. Not a candidate-9 level target, though the reproducible tilt is real.

NOISE-DOMINATED: a small, reproducible train-side under-production tilt (-0.102, same sign on all 5 seeds) sits well inside the tolerance 0.373 (level 0.102 = 27% of tol; P(a fresh train draw clips) 0.00). The outer clip on seed(s) [3] is dominated by split (rate_a vs rate_b) + single-draw excess, not the level. Not a candidate-9 level target, though the reproducible tilt is real.

BOUNDARY: a small, reproducible train-side under-production tilt (-0.157, same sign on all 5 seeds) sits right at the tolerance 0.185 (level 0.157 = 85% of tol; P(a fresh train draw clips) 0.34). Whether a given split clips is a near-coin-flip of split+draw noise on a real level offset -- the honest candidate-9 target.

NOISE-DOMINATED: a small, reproducible train-side under-production tilt (-0.036, same sign on all 5 seeds) sits well inside the tolerance 0.051 (level 0.036 = 71% of tol; P(a fresh train draw clips) 0.10). The outer clip on seed(s) [1] is dominated by split (rate_a vs rate_b) + single-draw excess, not the level. Not a candidate-9 level target, though the reproducible tilt is real.

BOUNDARY: a small, reproducible train-side under-production tilt (-0.046, same sign on all 5 seeds) sits right at the tolerance 0.047 (level 0.046 = 99% of tol; P(a fresh train draw clips) 0.49). Whether a given split clips is a near-coin-flip of split+draw noise on a real level offset -- the honest candidate-9 target.

NOISE-DOMINATED: a small, reproducible train-side under-production tilt (-0.060, same sign on all 5 seeds) sits well inside the tolerance 0.171 (level 0.060 = 35% of tol; P(a fresh train draw clips) 0.00). The outer clip on seed(s) [2] is dominated by split (rate_a vs rate_b) + single-draw excess, not the level. Not a candidate-9 level target, though the reproducible tilt is real.

question_3_rng_stability.finding: Every one of the six failing cells carries a small, reproducible train-side UNDER-production tilt (the model runs a few percent low on all of them in-sample, same sign on all 5 seeds), so none is pure draw noise around a matched level -- but they split by how close that tilt sits to the tolerance. LEVEL (tilt alone clips): none. BOUNDARY (tilt sits at/near the tolerance, P(a fresh train draw clips) >= 0.25 -- a near-coin-flip): ['share_widowed.75+|female', 'mean_lifetime_marriages|male']. NOISE-DOMINATED (tilt well under tolerance, the outer clip is split/draw luck on top): ['share_divorced.45-54|female', 'widowhood.75+|female', 'mean_lifetime_marriages|female', 'completed_fertility.c1970s']. So the outer FAIL 0/5 is largely draw/split luck around a model that runs slightly low across the board; the two cells whose reproducible tilt is close enough to the boundary to matter -- ['share_widowed.75+|female', 'mean_lifetime_marriages|male'] -- are the honest candidate-9 level targets, and they corroborate Q1 (the male marriage-count residual) and the survivorship stock. The registration's knife-edge share_divorced.45-54|female and the seed-specific widowhood.75+ incidence are NOISE-DOMINATED: real but sub-tolerance tilts whose clips are split/draw excursions, not level misses to chase.

candidate_9_implications: Q1: the marriage-count deficit is the reference residual (0.116/person of marriages with no datable/scoreable in-exposure event), not the modelled remarriage hazards (the simulation matches or exceeds the reference in-exposure count). A candidate 9 must represent that residual pathway rather than lift remarriage rates. Q2: the c1970s gap is a broad parity-progression miss, largest at first_birth_0_to_1 but declining monotonically with parity (not concentrated at 2+); it is not the [15,49] censoring (effectively shared) nor a timing shift. Q3: the honest level targets (reproducible tilt at/near tolerance) = ['share_widowed.75+|female', 'mean_lifetime_marriages|male']; the noise-dominated clips to leave alone = ['share_divorced.45-54|female', 'widowhood.75+|female', 'mean_lifetime_marriages|female', 'completed_fertility.c1970s']. All under the one-shot rule, registered only after citing this evidence.
